meta_title: For Freelancers: testing automation to Solve no portfolio (Beginner Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to testing automation and overcome no portfolio. A Beginner-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Freelancers: Build a Portfolio with Automated Testing (Beginner)
content.seo_description: Stop struggling with no portfolio. Learn to use Antigravity and Claude Code to implement automated testing that proves your engineering quality to clients.
content.answer_block: The 'no portfolio' trap is solved by demonstrating engineering rigor, not just UI flair. By implementing automated testing, you prove you ship production-ready code. Use Antigravity to scaffold robust test suites and Claude Code to execute them, turning your repository into a verifiable showcase of professional reliability for prospective clients.
content.intro:
You are staring at a blank screen, the silence of a client-less inbox deafening. You have the skills, but the prospective clients demand proof of work, and your portfolio is a ghost town. The frustration isn't just a lack of projects; it is the inability to prove your worth in a market saturated with amateurs who skip the hard stuff.

But here is the secret: clients don't just want pretty interfaces; they want systems that don't break. By shifting your focus to high-coverage automated testing, you position yourself as a senior engineer who values stability and scale. This isn't just about code; it's about building a narrative of competence that forces clients to take you seriously.

steps.title: Define the Testing Scope
steps.description: Identify the critical user flows in your project that require verification. This establishes the 'why' behind your testing infrastructure.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze this codebase and identify the 3 most critical user flows that require unit and integration testing to ensure production stability.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Identify critical paths in the current directory and list them in a markdown file called TEST_PLAN.md'

steps.title: Scaffold the Test Environment
steps.description: Use Antigravity to generate a standardized testing framework, ensuring your setup matches industry benchmarks.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Scaffold a Jest and Playwright testing environment for this project, including configuration files for CI/CD readiness.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Initialize a professional test suite using Jest and Playwright with standard configuration'

steps.title: Automate Unit Test Creation
steps.description: Generate comprehensive unit tests for your core logic modules to demonstrate code quality.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write comprehensive unit tests for the identified critical functions, targeting 90% branch coverage.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create unit tests for all files in the /src/utils directory and ensure they pass'

steps.title: Implement Integration Flows
steps.description: Simulate real-world user interactions to prove that your application components work in harmony.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a Playwright integration test that simulates a full user authentication and data submission flow.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create a Playwright e2e test script for the login and form submission flow'

steps.title: Configure the GitHub Actions Pipeline
steps.description: Automate the execution of tests on every push, proving to clients that your code is constantly validated.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a GitHub Actions YAML file that runs the test suite automatically on every pull request.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Generate a .github/workflows/test.yml file that executes npm test on push'

pro_tips: Always include a 'Coverage Badge' in your README; it is the single fastest way to signal senior-level engineering to a non-technical client.
Use descriptive 'it' blocks in your tests that read like business requirements; this helps clients understand the logic without reading code.
Mock external APIs to keep your tests fast and deterministic; flaky tests are worse than no tests.
Keep your test files alongside the source code in a __tests__ directory to maintain strict modularity.
Refactor your tests as you refactor your code; a stale test suite is a liability that signals poor maintenance habits.
content.next_steps: Once your tests are passing, add a 'Testing Philosophy' section to your portfolio site, linking directly to your GitHub repository to show the automated pipeline in action.
content.conversion_block: You now have the tools to transform a 'no portfolio' problem into a 'proven reliability' asset. Stop waiting for the perfect project and start engineering your own credibility. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
